purpose: Verify that swapping baseline/CoT grid representation from JSON (ARC Prize harness format) to ASCII-with-coordinate-headers (matching pipeline agents) does not materially affect single-call accuracy. Supports the architectural-lift claim by holding grid format constant across all architectures.

swing.interpretation: Within sampling noise; ASCII slightly worse but indistinguishable.
Mildly favorable to ASCII (+2.25pp task-level); within sampling noise on a single comparison, but consistent direction. CoT exercises the model more than baseline so format-sensitivity surfaces here.

decision: ASCII format adopted as canonical for baseline + CoT (matches pipeline agents). All architectures now consume training examples in the same ASCII-with-coordinate-headers format. The architectural lift reported in the paper therefore measures architecture, not prompt format.
